title: xanadu rich lentils indian puy-lentils paratha roll
description: a nostalgic and rich indian dish featuring silky puy-lentils wrapped in a crispy paratha.
instructions: heat ghee in a pan over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion until translucent.
add the ginger paste, garlic paste, cumin seeds, coriander powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt to the pan and sauté for 1 minute.
add the puy lentils to the pan and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
add water to the pan as needed and bring the mixture to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
warm the paratha dough by wrapping it in a damp cloth for 10-15 minutes.
divide the cooked lentil mixture into 4-6 portions depending on the size of the parathas.
roll out each portion of the paratha dough into a thin circle.
place a portion of the lentil mixture in the center of each paratha circle.
fold the paratha over the filling and press the edges to seal.
heat a non-stick pan or cataplana over medium heat and cook the paratha rolls for 2-3 minutes on each side or until golden brown.
serve the xanadu rich lentils indian puy-lentils paratha rolls hot with a dollop of raita or chutney.
